heading: Definitions
label: 4101 Definitions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large .5 U.S.C. 2302, , .July 7, 1958Pub. L. 85–507, § 372 Stat. 328In paragraph (1), the word “agency” is substituted for “department”. Reference to the “General Accounting Office” is omitted as included in “independent establishment” because of the definition in section 104.In paragraph (2)(B), the words “in the Department of Commerce” are omitted as unnecessary.In paragraph (6)(C), the word “agency” is omitted as unnecessary and to avoid confusion with the word “agency” defined by paragraph (1).In paragraph (6)(E), the words “individuals other than civilian or military personnel of the Government” are substituted for “an individual not a civilian or military officer or employee of the Government of the United States or of the municipal government of the District of Columbia” to conform to paragraph (5).The definition of “Commission” in former section 2302(4) is omitted as unnecessary as the title “Civil Service Commission” is fully set out the first time it is used in each section of this chapter.Standard changes are made to conform with the definitions applicable and the style of this title as outlined in the preface to the report.

Amendments1994—Par. (4). substituted “fields which will improve individual and organizational performance and assist in achieving the agency’s mission and performance goals;” for “fields which are or will be directly related to the performance by the employee of official duties for the Government, in order to increase the knowledge, proficiency, ability, skill, and qualifications of the employee in the performance of official duties;”.Pub. L. 103–2261982—Par. (1)(C). substituted “chapter 91” for “sections 846–852 or 856–859”.Pub. L. 97–2581967—Par. (2)(B). substituted “Environmental Science Services Administration” for “Coast and Geodetic Survey”Pub. L. 90–206

Change of Name“Government Publishing Office” substituted for “Government Printing Office” in par. (1)(E) on authority of , set out as a note preceding , Public Printing and Documents.section 1301(b) of Pub. L. 113–235section 301 of Title 44
Effective Date of 1967 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as an Effective Date note under .Pub. L. 90–206section 220(a)(1) of Pub. L. 90–206section 3110 of this titleDec. 16, 1967

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of Environmental Science Services Administration to National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, see Transfer of Functions note set out under .section 5541 of this title

Delegation of FunctionsFunctions of President under subsec. (6)(B) of this section delegated to head of each agency concerned, see section 402 of Ex. Ord. No. 11348, , 32 F.R. 6335, set out as a note under .Apr. 20, 1967section 4103 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: a Government corporation subject to chapter 91 of title 31;

children.label: F
children.text: the government of the District of Columbia;

children.label: A
children.text: an individual employed in or under an agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: a commissioned officer of the Environmental Science Services Administration;
“Government” means the Government of the United States and the government of the District of Columbia;

“training” means the process of providing for and making available to an employee, and placing or enrolling the employee in, a planned, prepared, and coordinated program, course, curriculum, subject, system, or routine of instruction or education, in scientific, professional, technical, mechanical, trade, clerical, fiscal, administrative, or other fields which will improve individual and organizational performance and assist in achieving the agency’s mission and performance goals;
“Government facility” means property owned or substantially controlled by the Government and the services of any civilian and military personnel of the Government; and

children.label: A
children.text: the government of a State or of a territory or possession of the United States including the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and an interstate governmental organization, or a unit, subdivision, or instrumentality of any of the foregoing;

children.label: B
children.text: a foreign government or international organization, or instrumentality of either, which is designated by the President as eligible to provide training under this chapter;

children.label: C
children.text: a medical, scientific, technical, educational, research, or professional institution, foundation, or organization;

children.label: D
children.text: a business, commercial, or industrial firm, corporation, partnership, proprietorship, or other organization;

children.label: E
children.text: individuals other than civilian or military personnel of the Government; and

children.label: F
children.text: the services and property of any of the foregoing furnishing the training.
